The obvious O(n log n) solution is to sort the array and look for adjacent duplicates, but the problem says you cannot modify the array.
The obvious O(n) solution is to use a Hash Set to track seen numbers, but the problem says you can only use O(1) space.
Because the array values are in the range [1, n] and there are n+1 elements, you can treat this array as a Linked List! The index is the node, and the value at that index is the 'next' pointer.
Since there is a duplicate number, multiple indices ('nodes') will point to the same value ('next node'). This means there is a cycle in our imaginary Linked List!
Use Floyd's Cycle-Finding Algorithm (Fast and Slow Pointers) to find the entrance to the cycle.
